steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Text 1 fundamentally questions whether genre classification is useful, since genres are “ambiguous, subjective, and simplistic,” and other concepts may better explain musical relationships.

steps.title: Match the framework to the choices
steps.content: The author would respond to Costa's benefits claim by asserting that genre classification may not be the most helpful framework—because improving something doesn't make it useful if it's built on a flawed premise. The other choices either raise concerns Text 1 doesn't discuss (A about listener preferences), or treat genre classification as fundamentally sound and valuable (C and D), both contradicting Text 1's skepticism. That matches choice B — B is correct.
